text: T- mobile is horrible.  The customer service and stores are never on the same page and the customer wastes so much time going back and forth when following their directive.

After I been patient and remained loyal for over 4 years...Over charge.... no service for more than a year and a half at my work places.... was told  by tech to change carriers and I received no compensation for non service periods... I’ve had agents yell at me... supervisors were not able to provide solutions the app would not work... glitches in the system caused payments not to go through resulting in suspension and restoration fees not waived

Don't be fooled by the run around they'll give you if you have a problem. They make it impossible to to get to the bottom of an issue. Their customer service is atrocious and they give their people no training, no power, no autonomy, just scripted responses that make no sense until you are exhausted. Last month my mother got cell phone service from T-Mobile. She requested auto refill. At the time she complained they gave her no paper work. One month later she gets a message that her service has been suspended. 

 I  go to a T-mobile and find out what is going on. I talk to Yarron. He looks the account up and tells me 
-our account has been suspended, nothing he can do.
I inform him that we were on automatic payment.
He tells me
-no we were not on automatic payment
I tell him that we were messaged by T-mobile that they received our money.
He tells me
Well, Mom’s account has been suspended. And suggests it was because she didn’t pay on time.
I tell him we did pay on time because it was AUTOMATIC.
He tells me
-no we aren’t on automatic payment.
I tell him we are and ask him
-Then why/how did an automatic payment go through if we aren’t on automatic payment?

I don’t know what he answered to that exactly but he clearly felt it was either our fault or perhaps the original T-mobile salesperson person.

He wasn’t able to hear me, believe me or understand me. I am not sure which. He stood, immovable and yet I wasn’t satisfied. My Mom paid her money, needed her cell tomorrow and he wasn’t interested in helping us unless we wanted to purchase a new service since the old one was suspended. 

I asked him to fix the problem and he told me it wasn’t his problem. I was confounded by that and showed my frustration by not accepting it. I said he and T-mobile had to do something. He told me that I was being rude to him. Which threw me. I wasn’t personally being rude to him but I was very frustrated. He told me that there was nothing he could do, there was no manager, that there was no action to be taken and I should leave.

So, here I was, knowing my Mom had paid for a cell service but had no service and was being told by this young man that it wasn’t their problem.

I videotaped him from here which bothered him and he stopped momentarily telling me I was treating him badly and I was being rude.

Okay so it was highly unpleasant. I went to my car and called T-mobile. 

This customer service person was difficult to understand, he mumbled, paused often, very difficult to follow but I think in retrospect he was saying “opted out” a lot. Basically he finally said that my bank or credit card or whatever was no good and I had to talk to the bank. I called my Mother and asked her to call the bank and drove home. 

We confirmed the money had been paid by the credit card so we called T-mobile again. 

We went through the whole thing again and explain the facts,

Mom signed up for T-mobile cell service and paid on December 10th. She asked for automatic payment for next month. Which she always does. Her credit card has been charged but the customer service representative Patrick told her that she’d have to wait for 72 hours to get her cell reinstated. 

Patrick tells us that my Mom must have:
Opted out of the automatic payment
Once again we explain if this was true, then why was her credit card charged.
He agrees to look at this and find that yes, there was a payment.
He claims WE must have paid the bill manually, not automatically and we were late so it will take three days to reinstate the cell.
We tell him we couldn't have been late to pay since it was t-mobile's own system that automatically charged my Mom's credit card.  

He repeats the same thing, up to 72 hours to get the cell back.

We ask for a Manager. We get Sam B. He listens and says that we must have paid manually. Also that his record show WE opted out of automatic payment TODAY just after we made a payment.
We tell him over and over that we did opt out, ever, and we did not make a manual payment, that it was automatic, proving we did not opt out. He tells us that we MUST have opted out because no one at T-mobile could opt out. Okay, we believe him but still, there remains the fact that we also did not opt out.  So what happened? We don’t know and T-mobile isn’t interested in finding out, they just want to believe, somehow, we did opt out but aren’t telling the truth about it. 

He claims that the automatic charge and payment on the credit card isn’t proof we didn’t opt out. We must have used a refill card to make a payment. We didn’t, we never had a refill card, didn’t know they existed and the payment came from a credit card.

We go around and around with Sam, maybe for 40 minutes? No apology, no acceptance of responsibility by T-mobile, no recourse offered. 

During all this he tells us that several times that T-mobile is undergoing an “unplanned service enhancement.” What the heck does that mean? More importantly, what does that mean and what does it have to do with our situation? Sam B. doesn’t say, he just repeats it like it is information that should clear everything up for us. 

He also frequently says that we are not the only people calling today. That lots of people are having trouble and calling. Not as a way of explanation that pertains to anything or within context. Except as a way of inferring we were being inconsiderate, like we were taking up time that other people might need. He kept repeating this, as if being told we aren’t alone meant we shouldn’t be so difficult, like T-mobile was an Emergency Room and lots of people were in pain and we shouldn’t complain since lots of people were experiencing problems due to the “unplanned service enhancement” that their engineers were working on. 

I asked Sam B. how T-mobile was going to make this up to us, that my Mom wouldn’t have service for up to 72 hours? Would there be a pro-rated credit? Would they change her start date. No, he said, no credit will be issued.  So, she is paying for a cell phone service she isn’t getting? I am not clear and T-Mobile isn't interested on how this happened or what we had to endure to try to get to the bottom of the situation.  

He even said that our issue has been noted but when I ask him what his notes say, he told me his notes will say that he explained the situation to us. 

They should have just said the truth. What would be so hard about saying something like; “We’re experiencing technical difficulties, we’re working on it, we hope to have everything sorted out soon and we regret any inconvenience this situation might be causing you. If your service was interrupted due to any issues on our end, we will be prorating your bill.” Not try to disguise the issue and blame us for their issues or make us take responsibility for their issues.

Just because T-mobile’s system is crashing isn’t our problem. I owe nothing to T-mobile. But T-mobile owes my Mother and I a lot. They promised my Mom cell service. Cell service she paid for. They did not tell us what was going on or even that they didn't know what the issue was. They didn't say they would investigate our issues. They did not apologize for making mistakes or admit to any mistakes. Instead they told us they were undergoing a “unplanned service enhancement”. This sounds like a way to make their problems sound like a good thing. But it is obfuscation, pure and simple. A way to excuse themselves. A way to not take responsibility but also a way to make customers have to deal with issues that aren’t their problem. We get the run around while I’m told I’m rude, my Mom is told her credit card or bank has no funds, we told (accused) of opting out when we didn’t.   We didn’t deserve the run around, the difficulty, stress and wasted time. I didn't deserve to be treated badly by Yarron. Everyone needs serious customer service training but more importantly, T-Mobile needs to stop treating customers so badly and get some integrity.

4651 w 6th st.  
Excellent service, very knowledgeable, really helpful with their suggestions.

Tmobile does not honor their promos. I checked via online chat and in an official tmobile store and my phone was said to be eligible. After tmobile received my trade in phone in the mail they said it was not eligible (after all) and will not be giving me full credit monthly. Scam.

T-Mobile Lies and Does Not Honor Their Contracts!!!!
So back in September I went in to the T-Mobile corporate store in Federal Way to inquire about the buy 1 get 1 promo on the Note10+ they were offering.  I was told we needed to add a single line on their plan and keep it for 4 months. I was told that my total cost would NOT change and actually would go down.  I was provided a written contract stating the total cost would be $150 per month for all 5 lines plus the cost of 1 phone and applicable taxes bringing the total cost to $189.50 for all 5 lines.  So.... I get my bill the end of September and it is almost $100 more per month.  I called and sent them a copy of the contract and they credited my account the overage and have each month until now.  They also said they had contacted the store and were working to get fixed.  I got my new bill the end of Dec and no credit was given.  I contacted their customer service via msg and now they say.... sorry we don't provide contracts and haven't for years (even though I have one) and will not honor that price as it is not something they offer.  What do I do?  So angry I can't think.
I got an excellent service from a T-Mobile expert THRU phone last night around 8:30pm , his name is Terrence, living in Philippine. He was so patiently and professional.

I have had service through all of the major carriers either for personal use or business use. For me, T-Mobile has been the most affordable and easiest to use. I love that I can turn my phone on internationally and have service and text and data are free in most countries. I also love the free Wi-Fi on Gogo equipped flights!

T-mobile went from the best company in NY to worst in no time. Worst customer service and lies,lies,lies. My service barely hanging at 1 bar... they doing upgrades... its been like this for over a year. How long should we wait to make reliable calls that wont constantly drop? Leaving t-mobile after being loyal to them for over 10 years.

T Mobil shows they cover Anchorage Alaska. I was put in a dangerous situation as I was driving following GPS. In below 0 weather my map and calling service went out as I entered Anchorage. No businesses were open at that hour. I finally saw man shoveling his driveway to use his phone to have someone drive 30 minutes to find me and get me to my destination. I immediately went to AT&T the next day and had to get another service to keep me safe in unfamiliar area.  When I called TMobil the agent said I could cancel but I would not get my discount on my next bill nor would I get the discount on my products I was paying for.  I offered to pay them in full so i could get the discount but they said it didn't work that way.  They also said they don't know which map im looking at because they don't cover anchorage.  I have a photo of their map and it shows they do.  So they are costing me money for their misleading ad. Im a senior and I feel abused and because of tmobile I was also put ina dangerous situation and they don't care.
Worst customer service and a truly dysfunctional organization. They lose your phone that you trade in and tell you to go F### yourself!!!!

It's ridiculous that a customer of 14 years with tmoble gets treated like we just did. We can go to a new carrier for $100 a month cheaper and get 5 new Samsung s10 and get a existing phone paid off. ALL TMOBILE DID FOR US WAS SELL US A NEW PHONE.......RUDICULIUS !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
THEIR CUSTOMER RETENTION REALLY SUCKS. TO MAKE IT WORSE THEIR OWN STORE EMPLOYEE SAID IF I WAS A NEW CUSTOMER I COULD GET THE SAME DEAK AS OTHER COMPANY

I have been a customer for 4 years. I wanted to add a line for my daughter. I called in and was told that my bill will go from $177 to $196 because of a promotion and a trade in promotion. 1st issue I didn’t get a return label for the phone. I had to call multiply times and they finally email one to me. I print it out and it says trade in amount $63. I was told the amount was $450!!! And then I see my new bill $302!!! I was told everything included $196. I am a reasonable person. If it was a few dollars difference from what I was told I wouldn’t make a big deal over it.  I have contacted T-Mobile numerous times and no one has a answer for me. This has been going on since the .2nd week of December. I am canceling my services with them and switching to another carrier. I was completely LIED to by the agent that set up my 3rd line in order to gain a sale

DO NOT I REPEAT DO NOT GET TMOBILE THEY MAKE YOU PAY FOR THEIR TEAMS mess UP. THEY OVERDRAFTED MY ACCOUNT AND THEN ARE NOW SAYING I OWN THEM WHAT THEY RETURNED WELL THEIR WAS NOTHING TO RETURN. I WILL NOT PAY FOR SOMEONE ELSES mess UP.

Just had one of the worst customer service nightmares with T-Mobile that I’ve ever had with anyone. We on Tuesday, December 31, 2019, tried to use their computer chat system to sign up for new service. It didn’t work, so we talked on the phone. The representative told us we could get 3 lines for $100 if we prepay, which we did, and said the 3 sim cards would be delivered on Friday. So yesterday, Friday January 3, 2020, the cards arrive but there are only 2. We call and get a recording saying we’ll get a call-back in one hour. That did not sit well with me. We have a problem and their routine is to get back to customers in an hour—what if we weren’t free then? In any case, they called. We (I say we because my wife and I took turns as this call went on and on) gave our info and our issue, talked a while, got put on hold, and then a different representative came on who knew nothing about our situation. We gave our info and told our story again, and again got placed on hold and then a different representative answered who knew nothing about our situation and we went through the routine again. This happened four times. We let the 4th know how unhappy we were. He assured us it wouldn’t happen again and put us on hold several times and kept coming back. He said over and over again, each time he returned, that the problem was that the payment was processed for two lines, not three. To T-Mobile, the fact we were mistakenly given two rather than three sim cards seemed an insurmountable problem that no one could possibly solve. Finally, we said, forget it—just give us a refund. We don’t want to deal with your company because we’ve never had such horrible customer service before. So he tried to refund our money. We are now 45 minutes into our call. He says he’s refunding it and then suddenly we’re on hold and a new guy comes on, a guy who knows nothing about the refund or our situation. He is very nice and apologetic, but he can’t in his dept. issue a refund, so he transfers us to person number 6, who does in fact issue us a refund (we hope—will have to see if it actually happens on our credit card). The call lasted an hour and 15 minutes. Also, we asked person number 4 to get us the supervisor and were told the supervisor would talk with us, but that never happened.
 I cannot fathom how T-Mobile is a large, successful company with customer service like this. Apparently, if a customer has what the reps consider a difficult problem (like the wrong number of sim cards being sent), they just hang up and let a new person deal with the troublemaker. Unbelievable.

After being with them for more than a year I had auto pay for the 1st. But i went ahead and made my payment on the 30th thinking they will register my pay and withdraw from my acct. But i was wrong they went ahead after i paid and took my money out again on the 1st when i called for  a refund the agant was very rude and told my money will be returned in 24 to 72 hours which it was a lie.  They later on told me it would take 3 business days to get my refund. I'm very upset.  They service is horrible and there was no helping me out with this situation.

I think everything has been stated below. I am unfortunately just confirming that the company is limited in its coverage and ZERO customer service. 
In a service business, that for the most part is commoditized were the only advantage is your coverage and your service, T mobile suffers greatly. 

After attempting numerous times to access the account online, I called in to shut off my father’s phone who passed away. He had service with them for years. After speaking to somebody who couldn’t do a thing except asked me a series of questions which I can appreciate for security reasons, even though I am the person listed on the account, I am his guardian, his power of attorney and now unfortunately his executor. After about 10 minutes of questions, a good number of them redundant, the service agent said she’d reach out and connect me to customer care to help me. When no one was available she mentioned she would have them call me back as soon as possible. At this point I still haven’t received a return call. I am assuming they’re waiting for the auto pay to be withdrawn in a few days.

HORRIBLE Service

The first thing on my list to do for 2020 is to remove myself from t-mobile and move back with Verizon or at&t. I went out with my husband and walked to the space needle to watch the fireworks. After 6 minutes and no firework I went on my phone to see what was happening and had ZERO service. It is REALLY SAD that the fireworks this year was sponsored by t-mobile as well as now them having safeco field... Why do I have ZERO service when you guys can afford so much.... I just don't get it. What if i had an emergency and needed to get in contact with friends or family... So for my safety I will be saying bye to a crappy company that advertises so much but shows so little to their consumers... see ya.

just now
In June I bought 2  apple iwatches and requested insurance coverage since it’s glass - and I thought that was that and a few months later I went back and got a iPad for my son I also asked for insurance coverage . Well my I watch cracked and was damaged I thought nothing of it In November I waited till after the holidays to take it in they said go to apple of a apple designated dealer I did and it wasn’t covered and then I called and then I found out it want covered only the iPad was and both my watches were not and I was paying 80.00+ extra a month for 2 iwatches and iPad and I was u see the impression everything was covered !!! I was upset and spoke to there service and a manager and bottom line I’m out of luck - basically I was LIED  TOO from the people at Tmobile and WHEN I hoped they would do the right thing they did not!!! So now I have a useless watch I’m still paying on but I now will get the other one covered under the insurance but when it’s all paid off I WILL change carriers and walk away from this service carrier . There is no accountability there . Also they told me I should
If checked my Tmobile account to see if everything was covered and it was my non due diligence that was also my fault basicly and to some degree they are right BUT saying that means I shouldn’t nor should any of you trust there sales team since u have to double check that they are doing there job accordingly since they can’t be trusted !!! 

Would I recommend TMOBILE - in the past I would have said Yes but after today ABSOLUTELY NOT!!!

And if u bought something check your statements to ensure u don’t end up like Me! Out of luck and still paying on a price of equipment that is useless !!
T Mobile never assist their customers. All I hear is I'm sorry I can't help you but their sure want you to pay your bill on time. NO NO NO

T-Mobile's "Fraud Department" canceled our service with no warning due to "unacceptable business risk" and refused to let us port our number, in direct violation of FCC rules and their own Terms & Conditions. We were good paying customers for many years with no late payments or unpaid balances. They refused to explain why the account was suspended, and it took two of us 8 hours of arguing just to get them to allow us to port the numbers out of the account, during which they lied to us so many times we lost count. They told us the only way to resolve the issue was to write to the Fraud Department at a P. O. Box address, which would have taken so long we would have lost the number due them giving us zero notice. Yet in reality, a customer service manager was able to grant the port request at the click of a button - but only after we threatened them with FCC and FTC action, which we have now taken anyway.

This could happen to anyone at any time. There was nothing unusual or suspicious about our account, apart from Chinese-sounding names. They will cut off your service with no warning, then a few days later you will receive a letter letting you know (how kind), and then they will try to keep your number. We will never buy service from T-Mobile, ever again.

I am extremely disappointed with their sales agent  Taylor giving me false information after I explained the reason I needed the line, told her I am only visiting the country for few days, gave me an assurance that there is full T mobile  coverage in my location all in the name of she want to sell a plan. At the end is not working Top it all the manager is the most cruel person have ever met in my life,  No problem solving skill, customer relation skill, I will suggest customer service training and he should learn how to think out of a box in resolving issue rather than looking for people help him resolve issues in a ridiculous way suggest I buy another phone at the store when I told him their network is not working at my location what dump way of thinking and this a manager. This is my first time using T mobile and once I'm done with the 1 month plan, The sim is going to the trash. I have concluded that T mobile is the worst network on planet earth.

I walked in to a T Mobil store in Tomball, Texas wanting to pay for two phones with cash. I was advised aggressively to do a promotion, buy one phone get the second half off. I told the rep that I would be moving out of the country in 6 months. She said that is not a problem still do the promotion and you can cancel at any time.T Mobil can only provide international service for 90 days otherwise we would've stayed with them. I used services for roughly 6 months and then paid off my phones in full, I was never late on a payment. I was assured, when canceling, by at least 3 reps my account had been paid in full and I was at a 0 balance. So I went on my merry way. A month later I got smacked with a 500 dollar fee!!! They said that since I canceled before 24 months I owed the other half of the promotion. I had dealings with at least 10 different representatives throughout the service and NEVER ONCE did they mention anything about me even being in a contract or much less a penalty. WHY did the first representative pressure me into this knowing I was going to cancel in 6 months and NEVER mention a peep about ANY of this??!! Also offering this when I had the cash in hand to pay for the two phones upfront! Why would I ever go into a contract knowing I was leaving the country in 6 months. When I called to inquire I was on the phone for over two hours being told basically "too bad." They offered to deduct some of the charge. How about the entire amount. I was lied to. So obviously T Mobil condones this type of bad business practice. T Mobil doesn't care about using shady sneaky tactics just to get a sale. They certainly don't care about customer service. I will never use their services again.

THE WORST EVER!! My problem escalated to a supervisor who said why don't you do us both a favor and go to the store, forget the discount we offered you today. Then HUNG UP ON ME! It's been 5 hours of my life I'll never get back. They record calls, but when I asked to hear my call, they put me on hold. THE WORST COMPANY EVER

You have to beware of everything you do with T-Mobile. We set up my husband's business phone account. We set up 4 voice lines and one tablet. Recently I called to find out why our bill was so much.They had a total of 7 lines 2 that were never authorized. When I questioned it they told me that I never questioned it before that's my fault. I had such a hard time the girl was so extremely rude! When I asked her to provide the proof that the lines for authorized she told me there was no proof. And it was still my fault that was then catch it sooner. Absolutely ridiculous!!!!!

The promotion a couple years ago when we switched is that they would buy out our AT& T plan which was about $700. It’s been 2 years & I suspected we never got the money. They are liars & don’t believe any promotion they say!! When I called to investigate it that far - they couldn’t pull up their records of course! I’m going back to another company because it was false advertising & cost us $700! Don’t sign up with them ... no morals in their marketing & I just hate that!! Verizon, AT& T - they’ve got to do better!
Been on hold with customer service for over an hour and still no one is answering. I would never use this company..

I went to the T-Mobile store in Bordentown, nj and was cordially assisted by Brett - who was not only efficient, but totally resolved my phone issue and even let me in the store a few minutes prior to store hour opening. I totally recommend this store and this rep if u need assistance. Was overjoyed to be able to get on with my day with my phone up and running again.

L. Safran (town resident)

Tmobile  scammed us through their promo. They took our old phone and pretends given us a free phone. They later kicked us out of promo due to there negligence.  The employee lied to make sales and later charging us to pay for full cost of the phone. Threatened to cut the service and report the fees to credit.  We asked for the old phone that they took since we didn't ask for new phone. They wouldn't give it back, they insisted on us paying for something that we can't afford. T-Mobile  rip off beware.

T-mobile is one of the big company and the worst company, they lied to me with a line for them in April and handing me my phone for an exchange, then they told me something that was not true and the next day I said that I didn't want the  line, and they told me that cell that was mine that did not give me back only gave me 68 dollars for a cell of a value of 400, the people of you have given me farkas information, so do not believe this company because they are very  fake?

So I walk into the door and no one greets me. Then as i stand there looking for direction no one still said anything but all employees watched me walk in. The lady right in front of me had a customer but they were not talking. She looked at me and didn't offer to say anything. I had to ask "is there a sign in?"  This place use to be different. The staff were more friendly. There use to be a sign in sheet. This is how confusion can easily start! 
T-Mobile store
Reisterstown rd 
Baltimore md

Seriously NO. They charge so many hidden fees, they don’t help you, their chat reps take AGES to r respond to you with tons of spelling and grammar errors. They do not help and cause reoccurring issues non stop. Not worth it what so ever. Please don’t get T-Mobile! Trust me. I’ve spoken to three reps and none of them helped at all. Their twitter reps also go extremely slow and take forever as well. Avoid avoid avoid. I worked for Verizon and we were not as terrible as this.
Very bad customer service every time when you call each individual agent tell me different things

I signed up for cell service.  To my dismay I discovered poor coverage at both my home and place of work. I live in a city of a million people.  I work in another city 25 miles away.  These are two locations I would expect all carriers to have cell signal.  Phone calls would not connect to me.  I had 0-2 bars of service and nonfunctional voicemail. 

I cancelled service which had no contract.  The representative said they were going to disconnect the line and service and that would be the end of discussion.  I had to ask what would be the charge.  She said, for 7 days of service, I owed 98+ dollars.  Without further conversation, she said they were going to disconnect the line and service and that would be conclusion. *click*.  

For that experience they got the truth on the internet.

I switched to Tmobile from Sprint two years ago...because it saved me $30 per month. Tmobile consistently overbilled me every month $2-3 at first i disputed it but it would take 1 hour and yelling to all hell to get it reversed - so i gave up...still was saving money...But now the coverage and speeds are too poor and they are no longer the cheapest around...See you later Tmobile....Good riddance...

U should really be training your employees..John Legere .... loser CEO

If I could leave zero stars, I would. T-Mobile will over charge you, charge you even once you have closed your accounts, and make it nearly impossible to get help fixing issues. If you are not the account's main user, they won't let you do anything related to the account. Even after the main user on our account authorized me, they still wouldn't answer my questions when I called in. To top all of this off, their service has been awful. If you can avoid them, you should.

T-mobile is terrible. I have been with them for 7 years and just keep dealing with the BS i guess because Im grandfathered into some of the older deals. The last few years they continue to add phantom charges that make absolutely no sense to me. I've asked several times for it to be explained why I'm paying $350+ for 2 lines!!! Yes, 2 lines!!! Its always well you were late this month, or the bill cycle started this month. They don't have any incentives to keep their old customers like discounts or deals.  And the service.... I live in the middle of my city and I have 1 bar. Its a fairly large city and should not have a problem getting reception. I have more calls drops than I can count. I will not recommend T-Mobile to anyone. I'm so disappointed because 7 years ago they beat out the competition. Now, I'm better off going somewhere cheaper for the exact same plan I have now.

My mother passed away, 2 weeks later I took her death certificate to T-Mobile on Mangrove Avenue in Chico California. The nice man called someone on the phone and I took a picture of the death certificate and sent it to them. It took about 45 minutes for him to inactivate her account over the phone and assured me that no charges would occur the following month. Of course they did take the money and overdraw her account as I was trying to close it. Now it's been almost 2 months and I can't get a hold of anyone in the billing department. Soooooo unprofessional!

many of you tell of trying to communicate with Tmobile on the phone. They have a lot of phone tag going on . They all say different things person to person. I was trying to learn of their system i wanted home internet after being moved from one person to the next all telling something different. I got their techs who had different rules then what i was told only to find out that for what i was willing to try i needed to have a active cell phone to sign up. I do not have a active cell but a active home phone that is internet based so they can't except that.  I also tried to find out what internet plans they had i got different answers for that. I was trying to change carriers because my bill which was based on a promotion was ending meaning i would pay more per month,so i was looking for a alternative not easy to find with few monopoly services in my area . Basically it's a run around. Some of you had similar experiences as me. Honestly we all should contact our District Attorney, Attorney General in our state to explain our experiences to them this is a industry wide problem and a case against this industry needs to happen we all are being used by this industry and it never will get better until we sue and fine them to death.  They are ripping millions of people off and these practices have to stop.

Go T-Mobile Call Center in Idaho!!! Wow!! Best call center in the world !! Everyone at this call center has been amazing!!! If every call center had the standard T-Mobile in Idaho have their would never be issues with customers! They got my wife and i Apple watches for a better deal then anyone else had including apple!! Followed up with me to make sure everything is all good! Thanks so much again!

Works only in dense populated states (New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Maryland, Connecticut, Massachusetts), does not work anywhere to West from North Carolina. You would have to look for a reception if you are not around a big town (more than 100k people). Simple mobile also didn't work there. 

If you are in the city of million people, it works just fine!

Alternatives are AT&T or Verizon wireless, especially if you're traveling much.
This review is strictly for JOHNNY everybody else should get fired he was the only one willing to help and solve my problem except just make up nonsense. PROMOTE THAT GUY!!

I really enjoyed my visit with Gabriel she was really friendly and helpful. Definitely a good sales person and nice lady! My problems were not with her.

So DISAPPOINTED in this BRANCH!!! T-Mobile needs to take a look at THIS store in Ocoee.  10959 W Colonial dr Suite 4 Ocoee Fl.
I had to leave florida to travel with my brother to Little Rock Arkansas for Medical treatments on the 1st of December. I am here until around Xmas depending on his response to the treatments,

So while here in Little Rock, I ordered the new Samsung note 10 plus 5G from a store here. It was the deal of add a line and get a phone of equal value free. While placing the order, the rep said the Aurora color phone would ship on the 6th and could be here at their store for me in a couple of days after that. The other phone ordered was to be Black and it showed that was not able to ship until the 19th of Dec. I was not sure where I was going to be at that time, so I asked if it could be shipped to a store by my house in Florida. 
I selected the above store and then the rep called the store to let them know it was being shipped there for me. They at first said they could not accept it as they were not a corporate store but a franchise, or words to that effect. The rep here told them they could as the store I was at in Little Rock was also a franchise and they had my other phone coming there to their store in Little Rock. 
So on the 6th I received Emails stating BOTH phones shipped and on the same day, they both arrived at the stores correctly that they were shipped to. I picked up my new Phone in Little Rock no problem,
I received a call from the store in Ocoee that they had my phone and it would be there when I wanted to come in  for it.

Wellll, I called today to see if I could let the person the phone was meant for come by the Ocoee store to pick it up without me there? I told them to let me know WHATEVER I needed to do, to make them comfortable it was me authorizing it, just let me know. Ahmed said to let him talk to his manager and he would call me back.

I got a call shortly after from Ahmed telling me that the manager had shipped my phone back to the warehouse a couple days earler as he did not know why it was there!!! I said then could they get the phone back? He said I would need to probably go to a store and have request it to be dropped shipped again. I said this should not be MY problem and they should do whatever THEY(T-Mobile) can do to get the phone back to their store. I was told by Ahmed that he would talk to his Manager and have the Manager call me back. 

That has been over 4 hours ago and  NOTHING!.  
All I can do is post this and send this to Corporate via the Customer Support as well at this point. 

More to follow.

Steve

If I could give zero stars I would! I wouldn't recommend T-Mobile to my dog.

Worse customer service ever.  First I want to say I used t-mobile service for over 2 years and the connectivity of the t-mobile service is average.  There was some poor connections and very poor connections when within buildings compared to verizon. However overall, it was not bad. Now the customer service and trying to get my phone carrier unlocked, was beyond horrendous. 

I first tried to unlock my phone through the t-mobile unlocking app, however it said my phone wasn't eligible for it. So I called t-mobile and they told me I should get a notification within 72 hours tops, and in most cases, within 24 hours.  So 24 hours pass by and I'm concerned because usually a response is given within 24 hours.  I call them up and they tell me guaranteed within 48 hours since 24 hours passed.  When the time was up, I called them and they told me they will escalate the situation or something like that and I should receive a response in 24 hours.  I call back, then long story short, the person told me I have to wait 14 days.  Now I'm getting mad.  I ask to talk to a supervisor and they put me on hold for years so I hang up and call again.  I talk to someone else that seem more helpful and he told me I should get a response back within another 72 hours. 

Finally after 2 weeks, they finally send me a notification to tell me that they can't unlock my phone because it's not a t-mobile phone.  Which is odd because I purchased a t-mobile version of the samsung galaxy s8 from Samsung website.  So Samsung is telling me it's a t-mobile phone and t-mobile is telling me it's not.  At this point I already realize my phone is not going to get unlocked so either I have to try resort to 3rd party unlockers or get an unlocked phone. 

*Update*
Called again, now another representative told me since I use the prepaid service, I must have 100 dollar balance in my pre-paid account and also my service must be activated. Said this is part of the eligibility requirements for the unlocking procedure. Why would I need to have 100 dollar balance and service reactivated with t-mobile when the phone is fully paid off through Samsung and no contracts for service?

Moral of the story, do not purchase a t-mobile locked phone because in the future if you decide to switch carriers, they will make it near impossible to impossible to unlock it. Just go with unlocked phones so you won't have to deal with these headaches.

They will be losing a perfect and loyal costumer.  I have three lines. I own all my phone. I paid my bill on time, if not early every month. I always paid off my upgrades withing 3 months. And now I will not recommend T-mobile to anyone that even wants a good phone company.

Switching to T-Mobile has not been and easy transition. The store I went to is a T-Mobile contractor and dose not know what they are doing. We switched 3 lines over to because they offered the free upgrade to the iPhone 11. The salesman looked at all the phones and said they qualify for the free upgrade so we decided to go ahead and switch to T-Mobile. After an hour and a half in the store he couldn’t complete the transfer of carriers. He told us to come back the next morning at 10 AM and he would finish us up. We showed up at 10 AM and he called in sick and none of the other employees knew what was going on. The salesman had to start over again with the carrier changeover. He looked at our phones and noticed that one had a chip and did not qualify for the upgrade. we end up just upgrading to the phones to iPhone 11. the next week they offered a free iPhone 11with any new line. We had to go back 5 times the first week to get my phone working correctly. Then I get a notice 2 weeks later that the two phones we traded in was not correctly looked at and Evaluated correctly by this T-Mobile contractor Store and we do not qualify for the full iPhone 11 free upgrade. Now I have to pay for T-Mobiles training issues with their contractors. Why do I need to pay extra when I was told everything was good when I signed the contract. T-Mobile should go after their contractor store not the customer. I wish I would have just stayed with Verizon.

I have had an incredible experience with T-Mobile's customer service agents. I had a situation where my new phone was shipped to me and my apartment didn't accept it due to my name not being on the package (this was my fault). The device ended up being returned. The customer service agent helped me reorder my device and ensured that even though the original promotion was over they would be able to credit my account the full value of the OG promotion. Once my phone arrived, I returned my old device without doing all the necessary steps. They helped me fix these and applied the promotion that I was promised thanks to the thorough notes that all the agents that helped me kept. I couldn't be more pleased with the help I received from T-mobile's customer service team.

As a loyal customer of T-mobile of over 11 years I am horrified to write a bad review. Recently one of the 5 lines on our account needed a phone replacement. We replaced the device through insurance that we pay to T-mobile and we were advised to return the damaged phone. The customer service rep that issued the replacement explained that since the phone would not turn on due to the issue of it not charging at all that the "find my I phone" feature could not be disabled and she would send out the replacement. When I put the damaged phone in the box to return I noticed in bold letters on the box it says do not return the phone without disabling the  "find my I phone"  or we would be charged a restocking fee. I called t-mobile and discussed my concerns with a representative who advised me to contact Apple. I contacted apple and I was told that we can only do a master reset if the phone can be found on our I cloud account which it can not. The phone however is not registering with I cloud and we can not get into the phone because it will not charge. I was told that If I send in a receipt for the phone as proof of purchase apple can wipe the phone. ( also I am not comfortable with sending a phone back to a company for refurbishing that has personal info, including pictures of my kids, addresses, bank info etc.)
I then contacted t-mobile for a receipt and they told me that they can not give me a receipt because the phone was purchased in 2015. 
So... Now I am told by T-mobile that because I am past the return window of 15 days that I owe them $650 for a phone that does not work because I could not send in the phone without wiping it according to their rules, they could not provide me with what I needed to do so and can't do anything to reverse the fee. Also every time I called in for help I spent 2 hours on the phone and got NO help. This is especially disgusting because I have like I said been a client for 11+ years and have 5 lines on our account! (NOT CHEAP) 
** By the way the phone that I am referring to is an I phone 6S..... (not valued at $650)

Hello Everyone,
	We have an account with 4 lines with t mobile under simple choice North America plan for almost 5 years. Let me share the three experiences with you with t mobile and please suggest me what I can do in this scenarios. 
	Scenario: 1:
		The primary account holder (let say X) for my account want to move from the line. We made a call to customer care and requested them can you make another person on the account (let say Y) and the primary account holder and give all the responsibilities to him. Then the customer care made all the changes and they mentioned you are good and there will be nothing linked to X for any case.
After calling 5 times and wasting time for almost 4-6 hours we got the above conversation and result.
	Scenario: 2:
		Now after we changed the primary account holder of account to Y. After one month we want to add the line on my account. I think we went to store and called customer care for almost many times and they mentioned sorry you cannot have the access to add this line as the account holder is Y. They asked my friend not use the mobile for almost 2-3 days as the t mobile systems are so fast and accurate. After going through lot pain and struggles somehow we got through this issue and line got added. 
They asked me to buy the sim and they did lot of process. I think I ended up paying almost $ 120 approx. from my pocket and the monthly bill has raised to 40$ for consecutive 3 months. I think this time I called customer care for almost 16 times and for almost 20 hours. Thanks for t mobile for their loyalty to a customer who has been with them for almost 5 years.
Scenario: 3:
 	After one month one my friend in my line has decided to go on business trip to INDIA. Before going to INDIA, he made a call to customer care and asked whether” he can use this service in INDIA?” The t mobile customer service has replied “we are the best in network coverage and we have coverage in almost 70+ countries and in which INDIA is also there. You can enjoy the trip. “As we did not believe in T mobile for the first time from different experiences we made a call again and asked then also mentioned the same.
He went to INDIA and he see no service on the mobile. We work in IT and all our work laptops needs the authorization code to log in. We always register our phone number to get the pin codes. As he don’t have the signal he is not receiving the pin and he not able to work. So he made a call to me, the primary account holder, Y, can you check with them? I told ok I will talk to them and get it sorted.
Now I made a call to customer care, and explained the situation.
Customer care: we can change this plan and we get his service
Me: ok can you do it for me now.
Customer care: Sure. Let me keep you on hold while I am working silently on this.
Me: Absolutely take your time.
I will be kept hold for more than one hour and say hello … hello... And no one replies. I have to end the call and call again. 
Here comes the funny part. There will be wait time to get the customer care and instead we can use the automated service which they call back in 5 minutes. They never calls me back in 5 minutes they take at least 1 hour. You get a call back and they speak in Spanish which I have no knowledge of it. After doing lot of research I came to know we have to press 1 to answer that call. Now the new customer care person gets assigned he again asks how I can help you. If t mobile records the messages and Write the memos of what we spoke with the customer service agents why can’t they refer to previous conversation.  Any ways I started explaining again as my friend need the service ASAP.  After listening all this they will say sorry we deeply regret and hard time we have given to you. He says the solution which doesn’t even match with the solution given by the previous agent. Ok can you do it for me again he told can I keep you on hold for 5 mints while I am working on it. That’s it I am on hold for again one hour and the same story repeat again. Trust me this happened 7-8 times and every time they gave me a different solution and they made me to wait for almost 7-8 hours on hold wasting my time and my friend time. 
Finally I think there is a person who called me from leadership and he asked what is going on with the account. I explained all he above things and I asked one thing can you please help my friend to get his service back? He explained me some steps that the process of making the primary account holder is not smooth. 
So you have to make the call with the previous primary account holder X and me Y and customer care they will send one time pin to you. They will take the approval from the primary account holder X and once done, we will run a credit check against you Y then the account will be moved from no credit prepaid to credit postpaid and you can enjoy the services. I asked him it will smooth process and he mentioned it should not take more than 1 hour. 
Now I got hold of previous primary account holder, me and customer care we did this process and the agent transferred me to another person. He told my SSN and ran the credit check. We are on the same page that the Simple choice North America plan is being moved to Magenta plan which is monthly bill of 160$ with postpaid credit account and with global service to all over lines. He told the credit check is approved and he transferred me to another agent to do activation process. Now the agent at the activation process asked me can you give me a minute while I am working in silent. He kept me on hold for almost for 2 hours and did not hear back. I ended the call the call and made the call again. They ask me the whole story again and I explained everything. After I explained they say the word can I keep you on hold for a while to do little research? That’s it I am again on hold for another 1 hour and story starts again. After doing this for 3-4 finally I got hold of one agent. She mentioned it is under review with higher officials and it will take time for almost 24-48 hours. I requested what about my friend. They all reply “sorry sir this is the process and system will not allow to do it?” As it became 2:00 am at night, I asked them can they check and let me know in the morning what is going on. Now when I woke up I see different plan on the account all together. I again made a call to the customer care next morning and told very harshly, as t mobile deserves after doing all this, what is going on with this account. 
Out of now where they again asks me we have to run your credit check and need the previous primary account holder approval. You already ran the credit check on my account. No sir we need to run and that is only the option we have.
Trust me with in less than 30 hours in which 7-8 hours of my sleep I made the calls to customer care for almost 31 times and was in call with them for almost 14-16 hours. Thanks to t mobile for treating me like this. After reading all this everyone has the question why are you still with t mobile. As my friend is in INDIA and I am in USA I cannot send the new sim from different carrier and t mobile is only option and the t mobile has taken advantage to it max. Some agents even don’t know how to speak and treat the customer. We made out mind clear once he is back from India we are changing to another carrier. We have 5 account with 4-5 each on it will be moved to another carrier as well. Is there anything I can file a complaint or proceed legally in this matter that happened with me and customer care?
 Why only concern is please let me know what I have to do to get the service to the person in the INDIA
